chunk_summary: This section focuses on implementing a gluten-free diet and controlling cross-contact for managing celiac disease in India, referencing ESPGHAN and ICMR guidelines for pediatric and adolescent care.
learning_objectives: Understand the principles of a gluten-free diet for celiac disease management in India.
Learn strategies to prevent cross-contact with gluten in food preparation and storage.
Explore guidelines from ESPGHAN and ICMR for pediatric and adolescent celiac disease management.
key_facts: Celiac disease requires strict lifelong adherence to a gluten-free diet to prevent complications.
Cross-contact with gluten can occur during food preparation, storage, or handling, necessitating strict controls.
ICMR guidelines emphasize early diagnosis and dietary management tailored to Indian food habits.
ESPGHAN recommends regular follow-up for pediatric and adolescent patients to monitor adherence and nutritional status.
reference_quote: ‘A gluten-free diet is the cornerstone of treatment for pediatric patients with celiac disease.’ — ESPGHAN position paper (2024)
